content:
**Maria Ho Captures 2025 National Heads-Up Poker Championship Title in Thrilling Finale**

*Las Vegas, NV – December 14, 2025*

Maria Ho has etched her name in poker history, claiming the 2025 National Heads-Up Poker Championship title after a dramatic run broadcast on Peacock this fall. Ho, a seasoned tournament pro and Poker Hall of Fame nominee, defeated Daniel Negreanu in a best-of-three final match that kept viewers and poker fans on the edge of their seats.

The return of the National Heads-Up Poker Championship, after nearly a decade-long hiatus, saw 64 of poker’s biggest names and rising stars battle in single-elimination play at the PokerGO Studio in Las Vegas. Ho, the first woman to win the prestigious event, navigated a challenging bracket that included victories over Phil Ivey in the quarterfinals and breakout star Landon Tice in the semifinals.

In the final, Ho dropped the first game to Negreanu, but rallied back with aggressive play and precise reads to win the next two games, clinching the championship and the $400,000 top prize.

“This is a dream come true,” Ho told PokerNews moments after her victory. “Heads-up poker is the ultimate test of skill and nerve. I’m proud to represent and hope this inspires more women to play the game.”

Negreanu was gracious in defeat, calling Ho “a true champion and a fierce competitor.”

The event’s triumphant return and Ho’s historic win are being hailed as a major moment for televised poker as it continues to surge in mainstream popularity.
